tasks.title: Prove native macOS recording feasibility with a short local capture spike
tasks.details: Phase 0 remains blocked as of 2026-03-11T22:21:47Z: the macOS smoke harness and recorder validation path are already implemented, but this March 11, 2026 run executed on Linux and cannot produce the two required ScreenCaptureKit smoke captures on real macOS hardware. No implementation files changed in this iteration because the documented exit criteria require direct macOS execution evidence, so there is nothing task-scoped to commit.
steps: Create a temporary Rust or Tauri spike that records the primary display for about 5 seconds
Confirm local file output, duration metadata, and repeat-run stability
Verify microphone capture can be layered on without destabilizing the base recording path
blockers: Phase 0 in IMPLEMENTATION-PLAN.md requires one successful macOS local MP4 capture and a second successful rerun before T001 can be marked done. This run executed on Linux (`uname -a`: `Linux claude-code-vm 6.8.0-101-generic #101-Ubuntu SMP PREEMPT_DYNAMIC Mon Feb 9 10:15:05 UTC 2026 x86_64 x86_64 x86_64 GNU/Linux` on March 11, 2026), so ScreenCaptureKit recording cannot be exercised here.

The local Rust toolchain is unavailable in this environment. `cargo --version` failed on March 11, 2026 with `/bin/bash: line 1: cargo: command not found`, so the smoke example cannot be invoked from this run.

The spike is intentionally macOS-only: `src-tauri/examples/recording-smoke.rs` exits with `recording-smoke is macOS-only` on non-macOS targets, and `src-tauri/src/recorder.rs` gates the recording path behind `#[cfg(target_os = "macos")]`. Without two real macOS smoke runs producing output-path, duration, and file-size evidence, marking feasibility as proven would fabricate the documented exit criteria.

tasks.title: Implement the Convex desktop recorder backend contract
tasks.details: The desktop-focused Convex surface is implemented in ../convex/desktopRecorder.ts and the renderer now routes upload preparation, completion, failure handling, and upload-target listing through that module. Refreshing convex/_generated so api.desktopRecorder.* can replace manual function references is tracked separately.
steps: Create convex/desktopRecorder.ts
Add listUploadTargets wrapper around the existing project upload target query
Add prepareUpload action that creates the video record and returns a presigned upload URL
Add completeUpload action that marks upload complete, resolves a share link, and returns canonical URLs
Add failUpload action that marks upload failure cleanly
Run Convex code generation and verify auth and authorization stay backend-owned

tasks.title: Scaffold the desktop React and Tauri application shell
tasks.details: Create the desktop app package, build configuration, renderer entrypoints, and native Tauri bootstrap described in the planning docs.
steps: Create the desktop package and TypeScript build config
Create the Vite and Tauri configuration files
Add renderer entrypoints and a minimal app shell
Add Cargo manifest and narrow native boot wiring for the initial command surface

tasks.title: Integrate Clerk and Convex auth inside the desktop renderer
tasks.details: Mounted Clerk and Convex providers at the renderer root, added a local Clerk sign-in screen for unauthenticated boots, verified authenticated Convex access with projects.listUploadTargets, and kept desktopAuth as a no-op fallback boundary because Clerk works directly in the Tauri webview.
steps: Create desktop Clerk and Convex provider wiring
Mount providers at the renderer root
Implement login handling for unauthenticated app boots
Validate that authenticated Convex calls work from the desktop webview
Decide whether a desktopAuth fallback layer is necessary

tasks.title: Implement native permissions and device enumeration commands
tasks.details: Implemented a minimal native command surface for screen and microphone permissions, capture display/window enumeration, microphone discovery, system-audio support detection, and typed renderer wrappers with browser-safe fallbacks.
steps: Implement screen and microphone permission status checks
Implement permission request and open-settings commands
Implement display, window, and microphone enumeration
Implement system-audio support detection
Expose typed wrappers for the command surface in the renderer

tasks.title: Build desktop state models and persistent user defaults
tasks.details: Added typed app lifecycle, recorder session, and localStorage-backed user defaults models; wired the shell and recorder controls to persist last project, microphone, audio/countdown settings, and post-upload defaults.
steps: Create top-level app state for boot, auth, permissions, ready, recording, uploading, complete, and error states
Create recorder state for selected project, target, microphone, countdown, and upload result
Create settings persistence for last project, last microphone, audio settings, countdown, and post-upload behavior

tasks.title: Implement the pre-recording setup flow for permissions, target selection, and project selection
tasks.details: Built a dedicated pre-recording setup route with permission gating, capture target and microphone pickers, project selection, route helpers, and persisted defaults. The renderer now queries desktopRecorder.listUploadTargets; generated api.desktopRecorder references still depend on a future Convex codegen refresh.
steps: Build the permission gate UI with direct actions to request access or open settings
Build capture target selection for displays and windows
Build microphone selection including a no-microphone option
Build upload project selection using desktopRecorder.listUploadTargets
Persist the last used project and microphone and prefer good defaults

tasks.title: Implement the native recording lifecycle and normalized stop payload
tasks.details: Wrap Cap-style instant recording behavior in a small command set that the renderer can drive without inheriting Cap's larger product surface.
steps: Implement startRecording, pauseRecording, resumeRecording, stopRecording, cancelRecording, and getCurrentRecording
Maintain explicit native recorder state ownership
Return normalized stop metadata including final video path, file size, duration, and optional thumbnail path
Connect recorder controls in the renderer to the native lifecycle

tasks.title: Implement native file upload streaming with progress and cancellation
tasks.details: Stream the recorded local file to the presigned S3 URL from Rust so large uploads do not block the renderer and progress can be surfaced immediately.
steps: Implement uploadFile to accept a local video path and presigned upload URL
Stream bytes in a single-part upload compatible with the current loam limit
Emit progress events the renderer can subscribe to
Implement cancellation and coherent error reporting

tasks.title: Build the renderer upload orchestration and uploading route
tasks.details: Coordinate prepareUpload, native uploadFile, completeUpload, and failUpload in one renderer-side flow with clear progress and guarded navigation.
steps: Create uploadFlow as the single orchestration point
Create the uploading route and progress component
Call desktopRecorder.prepareUpload before native streaming begins
Call desktopRecorder.completeUpload on success and failUpload on error or cancellation
Prevent accidental navigation during active uploads unless the user explicitly cancels

tasks.title: Implement the completion flow that defaults to share links
tasks.details: Make the default post-upload outcome immediate by copying the share URL, exposing browser and dashboard actions, and handling processing states cleanly.
steps: Create the complete route
Copy the returned share URL by default
Offer Open in Browser and Open Dashboard actions
Show clear processing-state messaging while playback is still being prepared

tasks.title: Harden common failure and recovery paths across the recorder flow
tasks.details: Close the fragility gaps that would force users to restart the app after expected permission, device, or upload problems.
steps: Handle screen and microphone permission denial cleanly
Handle missing microphone devices and disappearing capture targets
Handle upload cancellation and app close during upload
Enforce the documented 5 GiB file-size constraint before upload begins
Make failures recoverable without a full app restart

tasks.title: Add backend tests for the desktop recorder contract
tasks.details: Verify that desktopRecorder prepare, complete, and fail flows preserve loam's auth, upload, share-link, and canonical URL expectations.
steps: Add tests for prepareUpload
Add tests for completeUpload
Add tests for failUpload
Verify share URL and dashboard URL behavior under expected success paths

tasks.title: Add native smoke coverage for recording output and stop metadata
tasks.details: Added a macOS smoke example that records a short clip through the native recorder path, validates the stopped payload against on-disk output, and adds deterministic unit coverage for stop metadata validation.
steps: Add a smoke script or native example that records a short clip locally
Validate that the stop payload points to a file that exists
Verify duration and file metadata are populated as expected

tasks.title: Run end-to-end manual smoke validation for the macOS record-to-share flow
tasks.details: Validate the product as a whole rather than only individual layers so the v1 flow is proven on a real machine.
steps: Run display recording success flow
Run window recording success flow
Run mic on and mic off flows
Run upload success, cancel, and failure flows
Run permission denied recovery flows
Verify copied share links resolve correctly even while processing
blockers: Manual end-to-end validation for this task requires a real macOS machine running the desktop app, but this Codex run is on Linux, so ScreenCaptureKit permissions, native recording, upload, clipboard, and browser-open behavior cannot be exercised here.

Manual end-to-end validation for this task requires a real macOS machine running the desktop app. This Codex run is on Linux (`uname -a`: Linux claude-code-vm 6.8.0-101-generic x86_64), so ScreenCaptureKit permissions, native recording, upload, clipboard, and browser-open behavior cannot be exercised without fabricating results.
Use MACOS-FINISH-PLAN.md as the execution checklist so the macOS handoff captures smoke results, manual QA outcomes, and any remaining packaging blockers in one place.

tasks.title: Prepare the macOS desktop app for internal release use
tasks.details: Finish the packaging and release-readiness work needed to use the recorder beyond local development once the core flow is stable.
steps: Verify desktop build and packaging configuration for macOS
Confirm runtime environment variables and auth configuration are documented
Document the record-to-share workflow, known constraints, and deferred work
Ensure the app opens the right share and dashboard destinations after upload when defaults are enabled

tasks.title: Serialize native recording start so countdown cannot launch overlapping sessions
tasks.details: Added a `Starting` reservation state in the native recorder so `start_recording` claims the single-recorder slot before countdown and platform startup work begin, keeps that reservation through preflight and native start, and reliably restores `Idle` on failure. Overlapping start attempts now fail immediately instead of racing into a second native session.

tasks.title: Use a desktop-native external-open path for completion browser launches
tasks.details: Added a native `open_external_url` Tauri command that validates `http` and `https` URLs before handing them to the platform opener, exposed it through the renderer bridge, and switched the completion route’s automatic and manual browser launches to use that desktop-backed path instead of raw `window.open` or `_blank` anchors.

tasks.title: Refresh Convex generated API and remove manual desktopRecorder function references
tasks.details: desktopRecorder is implemented, but convex/_generated has not been refreshed in this checkout, so the renderer still uses manual makeFunctionReference bindings for desktopRecorder.listUploadTargets, prepareUpload, completeUpload, and failUpload.
steps: Configure CONVEX_DEPLOYMENT and run the normal Convex codegen flow from the loam repo
Confirm convex/_generated/api.d.ts exposes api.desktopRecorder.*
Replace manual desktopRecorder makeFunctionReference bindings in src/App.tsx and src/lib/uploadFlow.ts with generated api.desktopRecorder references
Run bun test /home/agent/loam/convex/desktopRecorder.test.ts
Run bun run build
blockers: Convex codegen cannot be refreshed in this checkout until CONVEX_DEPLOYMENT is configured against the target loam backend.
